data.title: EPD: Permian-driven U.S. supply growth, robust global demand, and export expansion define the outlook
data.description:
U.S. hydrocarbon growth will be led by the Permian Basin, with disciplined production and significant gains in wet gas and NGLs. Global demand, especially for petrochemicals and LNG, remains robust, and new pipeline capacity could unlock additional supply. Technology and export opportunities continue to drive the outlook.

data.title: Australia's EBR Systems flags possible unauthorised access to patient health data
data.description:
Medical technology firm EBR Systems on Wednesday said a cybersecurity incident detected in February may have resulted in unauthorised access to a small amount of personal health information.

The company said it became aware of a network disruption around February 13 that affected certain systems, triggering an investigation with the assistance of third‑party computer forensics specialists. It said it had to alert patients and post details on its website.

The incident underscores Australia's growing exposure to cybersecurity threats , with the country seeing a sharp rise in data breaches and ransomware attacks over the past five years, including at healthcare, financial and government‑linked organisations.

The review found that certain information stored on its network was subject to unauthorised access, though the company said its assessment remains ongoing and the volume of data involved appears limited based on the samples reviewed so far.

The incident did not cause any material disruption to EBR's operations and is not expected to have a material impact on its financial results, the company said, adding that it holds cybersecurity insurance that covers costs related to the breach.

data.title: Micron Technology EVP, Chief Business Officer Sold Shares Worth Over $10M
data.description:
Sadana Sumit, an officer serving as EVP and Chief Business Officer, reported a sale of Micron Technology common stock on 2026-04-10. The shares were sold at a weighted average price of $421.35 per share, with individual trades ranging from $421.00 to $421.865 per share. The total sale amount reported was $10,112,400. Following the reported transaction, Sadana Sumit directly owns 224,021 shares of Micron Technology common stock.

data.title: China's Huaqin seeks to raise nearly $581 million in HK listing amid market volatility
data.description:
China-based technology firm Huaqin launched a Hong Kong share sale on Wednesday, aiming to raise up to HK$4.55 billion ($580.85 million), as the city's capital markets continue to attract interest despite war-driven market volatility.

The Shanghai-listed electronics manufacturer said it is offering 58.5 million shares for the global offering at up to HK$77.70 per share.

Huaqin's listing in Hong Kong follows a slew of other Chinese technology firms choosing the Asian financial hub, which cemented its status as the world's top IPO destination last year.

Earlier this week, Shenzhen-listed Victory Giant launched a Hong Kong share sale, seeking to bring in HK$17.5 billion in capital, which is set to be the largest equity offering since the Iran war started more than a month ago.

Meanwhile, battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology , which held the crown for the world's largest listing in 2025 following a $4.6 billion Hong Kong raise, is considering a near $5 billion fresh share sale, two sources with knowledge of the matter said.

Huaqin expects the final offer price for the listing to be released no later than April 22. The shares are due to start trading on April 23 under the stock code 3296.

($1 = 7.8334 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: China-Backed Yancoal Australia to Kestrel Coal Mine Stake For Up to $2.4 Billion — Update
data.description:
By Rhiannon Hoyle

Yancoal Australia said it has agreed to buy an 80% stake in the Kestrel steelmaking coal mine in Australia's Queensland state for up to $2.4 billion, bolstering its position as one of the country's top coal miners.

Yancoal, which is majority owned by China's Yankuang Energy Group, will acquire the stake from private equity firm EMR Capital and Adaro Capital, a unit of Indonesia's PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia, the coal miner said in a statement to Australia's stock exchange.

The deal includes an upfront cash payment of $1.85 billion upon completion, targeted for toward the end of the third quarter of 2026, Yancoal said. It also includes payments valued at up to $550 million in total over five years, linked to average benchmark prices.

The Kestrel mine, located in Queensland's Bowen Basin, is one of the largest underground metallurgical-coal operations in production in Australia. Coal from the mine is sold mostly into Asian steelmaking markets.

The acquisition is "a strong strategic fit" for Yancoal, said Chief Executive Sharif Burra. It will give Yancoal greater scale and a more diversified business, by adding more steelmaking coal to its business, which relies mostly on thermal coal used in power generation for its profits.

"The acquisition positions us to deliver greater value to our shareholders and consolidates Yancoal's position as a leading Australian coal miner," said Burra.

Yancoal said it intends to fund the acquisition with its own cash, a $1.2 billion loan facility and cash flow generated by the enlarged business.

EMR and Adaro bought the stake in 2018 from giant miner Rio Tinto for $2.25 billion. Japan's Mitsui owns the remaining 20% stake in the operation.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: Treasury chief says US growth may exceed 3% or 3.5% this year despite Iran war
data.description:
By Kanishka Singh

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Tuesday the underlying U.S. economy remained strong and that growth could still exceed 3% or 3.5% this year despite the impact of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

Bessent has cast cuts in global growth forecasts and higher inflation projections by the International Monetary ‌Fund and World Bank as an overreaction. The IMF on Tuesday cut its growth outlook due to Iran war-driven energy ​price spikes.

"I think the underlying economy remains strong," Bessent said during the WSJ Opinion Live event in Washington.

"I do think that the growth could easily exceed 3%, 3.5% this year, still."

The war has raised oil prices and shaken markets globally. It has led to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz . Roughly 20% of global oil and natural gas exports were shipped through the strait before the war.

Bessent also commented on U.S. tariffs on other nations, saying they could be back in place to previous levels by July after the Supreme Court ​ruled in February that President Donald Trump overstepped his authority in imposing sweeping global duties under an emergency law.

"The tariff could be back in place at the previous level by beginning of July," he said, referring to options being pursued by the Trump administration such as probes under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974.

data.title: Declining Interest in Basketball Is Hurting Nike, Analysts Say — Market Talk
data.description: Nike's waning relevance is partially due to its focus on basketball, UBS analysts say in a note. Basketball has historically been an area of strength for Nike, with the company using the category to define itself as the best, coolest sports brand with partnerships with star athletes such as Michael Jordan, Kobe Bryant and LeBron James, the analysts say. But today, consumers don't seem as focused on professional basketball, they say. NBA Finals TV ratings have been lackluster in recent years, and college basketball is producing fewer stars today versus the past, they say. "Nike's superpower is based in part on being able to maintain an image of being the best sports brand despite selling a lot of commodity products. Basketball was a vehicle for being able to do that," they say. "It's unclear whether Nike can use basketball in the same way today." (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Canada Suspends Fuel Tax as Iran War Pushes Up Gas Prices — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Dubé

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said his government will suspend a fuel tax after a rise in prices at the pump due to the war in Iran. Starting Monday, Carney said, gasoline prices will fall by 10 cents a liter, while diesel prices will fall by four cents a liter. The fuel tax will be suspended until Labor Day in early September. The cut is expected to cost 2.4 billion Canadian dollars, equivalent to roughly $1.7 billion.

Carney called the move a "responsible temporary measure" to help Canadians hurt by higher fuel prices. Gasoline prices have increased about 27% in Canada since the start of the war, according to figures published by the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
